analyst_note: Q1 2025: Record-breaking quarter (2,289 victims) driven largely by Cl0p's Cleo file transfer software campaign, which bulk-listed victims. 8Base takedown (4 arrested, Thailand — Feb 2025). Phobos affiliates (Berezhnoy, Glebov) charged. Payment rate continued declining. Chainalysis 2025 annual total ~$820M; Q1 likely front-loaded. LockBit 4.0 announced but failed to materialize as major operational rebrand.

a1._label: Russian Domestic Actions Against Ecosystem Actors
a1.notes: No documented RU domestic actions Q1 2025. LockBitSupp (Khoroshev) remains in Russia, unsanctioned domestically. LockBit 4.0 announcement (countdown timer set for Feb 3, 2025) — no RU action. Consistent passive tolerance.

a2._label: Time Lag: Referral to Observable Russian Action

a3.notes: Populate from analyst model. Geopolitical context: Ukraine conflict ongoing — RU incentive to maintain cyber criminal capacity remains elevated. No signals of protection relationship disruption.

events.name: 8Base Ransomware — Operation (4 Arrests, Thailand)
events.notes: FBI/Europol arrested 4 suspected 8Base leaders in Thailand. 27 servers seized. 8Base had victimized 1,000+ organizations. Score: 7 — mid-tier group, leadership arrests with international cooperation, meaningful infrastructure disruption. Not RU/CIS core ecosystem.

events.name: Phobos Affiliates — Berezhnoy and Glebov Charged
events.notes: Roman Berezhnoy and Egor Glebov (Russian nationals) charged with operating Phobos RaaS. 100+ servers disrupted. $16M+ in ransom payments. Score: 6 — affiliate-level actors (not core RaaS operators), criminal charges filed but subjects likely in Russia, limited short-term operational impact.

b1.notes: Q1 2025 marks the first criminal charges against identified Russian nationals for ransomware operations (Phobos). Symbolically significant but operationally limited — subjects in Russia, no extradition mechanism.

b2.notes: 8Base takedown caused affiliate movement. Phobos charges elevated forum anxiety. Multiple new brands emerged (Anubis, VanHelsing, BlackLock, others). Cl0p Cleo campaign drove record victim count — separate from RaaS ecosystem dynamics. DragonForce cartel model announced. ANALYST INFERENCE — elevated confidence given documented LE actions.

c1._label: Ransom Payment Volume vs. Victim Count
c1.crypto_price_note: BTC ~$90K-$106K in Q1 2025 — high crypto prices inflate nominal USD payment figures
c1.source: Chainalysis 2026 Crypto Crime Report (2025 annual ~$820M); ransomware.live Q1 2025 = 2,289 victims (Check Point Research Q1 2025)
c1.notes: CREDIBILITY NOTE: Payment volume is ANALYST ESTIMATE. Q1 2025 victim count (2,289) is heavily inflated by Cl0p's Cleo campaign bulk listings — not representative of true attack tempo; many Cleo victims were listed simultaneously. Divergence signal holds: payments declining while victim count reaches record. Chainalysis notes 2025 payment rate potentially at all-time low of 28%.

nh_reports.notes: Confirmed IC-reported payments. Subset only — not representative of full ecosystem payment volume. Use for trend and family-level analysis.

c2.notes: Payment rate continued downward trend from Q4 2024's 25%. Populate sector data from Coveware Q1 2025 quarterly report. Cl0p Cleo victims primarily commercial/professional services — sector payment behavior distinct from ransomware-encrypted incidents.

c3.source: ransomware.live; Check Point Research Q1 2025; 8Base takedown Feb 11 2025
c3.notes: 2,289 victims is Q1 2025 record but driven by Cl0p bulk listings — interpret with caution. 8Base leak site seized Feb 11 — group had not relaunched within 90 days (degraded). Post volume historically high but structurally distorted by Cl0p methodology.
